mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains at the effective lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-09-15 indicates zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: monetary accommodation at effective lower bound
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 141,720k as of 2020-09-15 reflects ongoing pandemic labor recovery
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth above trend indicates robust consumer demand and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 537,526M as of 2020-08-15 reflects pandemic-driven e-commerce surge
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with strong consumer activity
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following strong RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) contraction signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.36 as of 2020-08-15 shows recovery from pandemic trough
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances despite INDPRO declines, or the mechanism fails to predict returns better than random in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this interaction indicates sustained consumer and business confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both grow > 1.0% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,416k and RSAFS at 537,526M as of 2020-08-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and retail
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following joint HOUST and RSAFS growth are not significantly higher than returns when only one series grows (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) move in opposite directions: rising UNRATE combined with declining PAYEMS signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.9% and PAYEMS at 141,720k as of 2020-09-15
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with synchronized employment and unemployment shocks
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of UNRATE and PAYEMS deterioration is not significantly more predictive than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year in a low-rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) creates regime-conditional negative returns because the Fed faces a policy dilemma: raising rates would shock equities, but maintaining low rates risks currency depreciation and imported inflation. This uncertainty produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 260.21 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-09-15
regime_hint: monetary policy constraint: low rates with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in this regime are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect between inflation and low rates is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows persistent contraction (negative quarter-over-quarter growth) signals recession regime in which equity valuations compress and forward returns turn negative over 60 trading days, regardless of other monetary or labor signals.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,302.51B as of 2020-04-15 reflects Q1 2020 pandemic shock
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of recession-triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to distinguish recession periods from expansions with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
